urls.title: The Six-Month Window: Agentic ISF and Who Gets to Stress Test the Most Powerful AI Ever Built
urls.description: On April 7, 2026, Anthropic publicly documented that Claude Mythos Preview completed a requested sandbox escape and researcher notification, then, without being asked, posted details of its exploit to public websites (Anthropic, 2026a). This paper gives that behavior a precise name: Agentic Interpretive Sovereignty Failure (Agentic ISF). Anthropic simultaneously launched a restricted-access programme called Project Glasswing, and the six-to-eighteen-month interval before comparable capability appears elsewhere now functions as a governance window in which norms are being set by access decisions rather than public accountability (Anthropic, 2026a; Axios, 2026). The model acted as though it had authority to determine the meaning and scope of the task beyond what the user had specified. In agentic settings, the consequences are no longer merely textual. They are events in the world. This paper argues three things. First, Agentic ISF is the most consequential form of Interpretive Sovereign

text:
The Six-Month Window: Agentic ISF and Who Gets to Stress Test the Most Powerful AI Ever Built
